pergunta: AnyAirlines wants a no-code integration that creates a record in SAP whenever a new Opportunity is closed in Salesforce. Which trigger should be used in Composer?
opcoes: Scheduler trigger with periodic polling of Salesforce
HTTP Listener trigger exposed for an external caller
A manually started flow run from Composer
justificativa: A Salesforce event trigger is the right choice because the flow must start automatically when the Opportunity is closed. Composer is well suited to this kind of no-code, event-driven SaaS integration.
correta_texto: System event trigger on Salesforce

pergunta: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) needs a flow that runs every night at 02:00 to sync inventory from Google Sheets into Salesforce. Which trigger is most appropriate?
opcoes: A row-change event trigger on Google Sheets
HTTP Listener triggered by an external scheduler
A manually started flow run every night
justificativa: A scheduler is the right trigger when the integration must run at a fixed time every day. In this case, configuring it for 02:00 matches the nightly synchronization requirement.
correta_texto: Scheduler trigger configured for 02:00

pergunta: The scenario states: “Business admins must configure integrations between Salesforce, Slack and ServiceNow without writing code.” Which tool is the exam most likely expecting?
justificativa: MuleSoft Composer is designed for no-code integrations using prebuilt SaaS connectors, triggers, and simple mappings. The scenario states: “Business admins must configure integrations between Salesforce, Slack and ServiceNow without wr... It is typically preferred over custom code when requirements are straightforward and speed-to-delivery matters.
correta_texto: MuleSoft Composer

pergunta: NTO wants to minimize unnecessary data transfer when using Composer to write to ServiceNow. What is the recommended practice?
opcoes: Select only the fields relevant for the flow
justificativa: The best practice is to select only the fields the flow actually needs. That keeps the integration simpler, reduces payload size, and avoids unnecessary mapping work.
correta_texto: Select only the fields relevant for the flow

pergunta: A flow needs to process every line in a collection returned from Workday. Which Composer element should be used?
justificativa: In MuleSoft Composer, a For Each loop is used to iterate over a collection and execute the configured actions for every item. A flow needs to process every line in a collection returned from Workday.
correta_texto: For each loop

pergunta: A candidate solution maps a single record from Salesforce directly into a list field in another system, without iteration. What problem is most likely?
opcoes: Composer will silently fix the mapping
Only the first item will be processed or the mapping will fail
All records will be processed correctly
Composer will automatically create a For Each
justificativa: If a target step expects a list but the flow does not iterate correctly, Composer may process only one item or fail the mapping. Collections should be handled explicitly with a For Each loop when needed.
correta_texto: Only the first item will be processed or the mapping will fail

pergunta: A scenario requires concatenating first name and last name into a single 'FullName' field inside Composer. Where should this be done?
opcoes: In the Formula Editor using string functions
In RPA before sending to Composer
justificativa: This kind of transformation belongs in Composer's Formula Editor, where string functions can combine fields into the format required by the target system.
correta_texto: In the Formula Editor using string functions

pergunta: A business user needs to add 30 days to the current date to calculate a due date. Which category of Composer functions is most relevant?
opcoes: Date functions such as ADD DAYS
justificativa: Date functions are the right feature when the flow must calculate due dates or shift dates by a fixed interval. ADD DAYS is a typical example of that pattern.
correta_texto: Date functions such as ADD DAYS

pergunta: AnyAirlines wants to test a Composer flow that is triggered by a new record in Salesforce. What is the correct way to use Test Mode?
opcoes: Activate Test Mode and perform the real triggering action within the time window
Test Mode is not available for event-based triggers
Use only mocked systems; real data is not allowed
justificativa: For event-based triggers, Test Mode works by waiting for the real event to happen during the test window. The correct approach is to enable Test Mode and then perform the triggering business action.
correta_texto: Activate Test Mode and perform the real triggering action within the time window

pergunta: A scenario explains that Composer Test Mode uses real systems and can create real records. What is the main implication for the exam?
opcoes: It is safe to use in production with any data
Test Mode must be used only in non-production or with test data
Test Mode uses only mock data
Test Mode cannot show run history
justificativa: Because Test Mode uses real connected systems, it can create or update real records. That is why it should be used in non-production environments or with carefully controlled test data.
correta_texto: Test Mode must be used only in non-production or with test data

pergunta: A flow failures when calling an external system due to a missing required field. What is the expected behavior of Composer?
opcoes: It retries automatically with different field values
It stops at the failing step and marks the run as failed
It skips the step and continues silently
It corrects the field mapping automatically
justificativa: If a required field is missing, Composer typically fails at that step and marks the run as failed. It does not silently fix the data problem for you.
correta_texto: It stops at the failing step and marks the run as failed

pergunta: NTO wants to understand what data passed through each step of a failed run. Where should they look?
opcoes: In the operating system event viewer
In Composer Run History, inspecting data pills for each step
justificativa: To inspect what happened in a failed execution, the team should use Composer Run History and review the values passed through each step. That is the main troubleshooting view for Composer flows.
correta_texto: In Composer Run History, inspecting data pills for each step

pergunta: A candidate claims that Composer can replace all Anypoint APIs because it can call many systems directly. Why is this not aligned with best practices?
opcoes: Composer cannot call any external system
Composer is intended for simpler, low-to-medium complexity integrations, not full API-led architectures
The exam does not treat Composer as a replacement for API-led architecture
justificativa: Composer is best for simpler no-code integrations, not for replacing reusable, governed API-led layers. Complex shared capabilities still belong in Anypoint APIs.
correta_texto: Composer is intended for simpler, low-to-medium complexity integrations, not full API-led architectures

pergunta: The scenario requires Composer to be invoked from an external, custom web application on demand. Which trigger should be used?
opcoes: System event trigger fired by a connected SaaS app
Scheduler trigger combined with periodic polling
A manually started integration run from Composer
justificativa: If an external application must trigger the flow on demand, an HTTP Listener trigger is the appropriate entry point because it allows the flow to be invoked programmatically.
correta_texto: HTTP Listener trigger

pergunta: A business admin asks which environment is recommended to build and test new Composer flows before moving to production. What's the best answer?
opcoes: In a sandbox or dedicated non-production environment
In any org, there is no difference
justificativa: New Composer flows should be built and tested in a sandbox or other non-production environment so real business data and production processes are not affected during development.
correta_texto: In a sandbox or dedicated non-production environment

pergunta: NTO has a flow that must apply different tax rates depending on the country of the customer. Which Composer feature should be used to branch the logic?
justificativa: An If/Else block is the right choice when the flow must branch based on conditions such as customer country. It is the standard way to implement simple decision logic in Composer.
correta_texto: If/Else block

pergunta: A scenario describes receiving a list of order items from Salesforce and needing to create one record in an ERP system for each item. Which structure is most appropriate?
opcoes: For each loop around the ERP creation step
justificativa: A For Each loop is the right structure when the flow receives a collection and must create one record per item in the target system. Without the loop, the list will not be processed correctly.
correta_texto: For each loop around the ERP creation step

pergunta: AnyAirlines wants to normalize customer names by trimming spaces and converting them to upper case before sending them to an external system. Which function combination is correct?
justificativa: TRIM removes unwanted spaces and UPPER standardizes the value in uppercase. Together, they match the normalization requirement described in the scenario.
correta_texto: TRIM + UPPER

pergunta: A flow must calculate a due date 10 days after the invoice date and send it to another system. Which Composer feature handles this?
opcoes: Date functions in Formula Editor (e.g. ADD DAYS)
justificativa: This is a standard Formula Editor use case. Date functions such as ADD DAYS let the flow calculate the derived due date before sending it to another system.
correta_texto: Date functions in Formula Editor (e.g. ADD DAYS)

pergunta: A candidate suggests storing complex JSON in a text field and parsing it manually in another system. Which alternative better matches Composer best practices?
opcoes: Use structured mapping with data pills instead of opaque JSON strings
Always store data as CSV in text fields
justificativa: Structured mapping with data pills is preferable because it keeps the integration explicit and maintainable. Hiding structured data inside opaque strings makes downstream processing more fragile.
correta_texto: Use structured mapping with data pills instead of opaque JSON strings

pergunta: A scenario describes frequent failures because values sent to the destination system are null. What is the best way to handle this in Composer?
opcoes: Use If/Else blocks to validate data before calling the destination
Disable validation in the destination system
justificativa: The right approach is to validate the data before sending it. If/Else blocks help prevent avoidable failures by checking required values before the destination call happens.
correta_texto: Use If/Else blocks to validate data before calling the destination

pergunta: NTO wants to see exactly which values we passed to a connector action during the last run without re-executing the flow. Where can they find this?
opcoes: Composer Run History, expanding the specific execution
justificativa: Composer Run History lets the team inspect a past execution and see exactly what values were passed in each step. That is the right place to investigate a specific run without rerunning the flow.
correta_texto: Composer Run History, expanding the specific execution

pergunta: In Test Mode, how long does Composer typically wait for the trigger event before timing out?
opcoes: It does not wait; it runs immediately
justificativa: Composer Test Mode typically waits only for a limited time for the trigger event to happen. The best answer here is the approximate 10-minute window described in the exam-style scenario.
correta_texto: Approximately 10 minutes, depending on configuration

pergunta: AnyAirlines wants to reuse the same Composer connection to Salesforce across several flows. What's the best practice?
opcoes: Create a new connection in every flow
Use shared connections configured once and referenced by several flows
Store credentials directly in each step
justificativa: Shared connections are the right pattern when multiple flows need the same system access. They reduce duplicate setup and make administration easier.
correta_texto: Use shared connections configured once and referenced by several flows

pergunta: A candidate solution catches a failed call to an external system and sends a descriptive error message to a Slack channel. What does this represent?
opcoes: A valid pattern for basic error notification
justificativa: This is a valid basic notification pattern. Composer can be used to alert teams when a step fails, even though deeper resilience patterns may belong in APIs for more complex scenarios.
correta_texto: A valid pattern for basic error notification

pergunta: A scenario compares two implementation options, and only Option B preserves validation and safer processing. Which should the candidate choose?
justificativa: Option B is stronger because it preserves validation before data moves downstream and avoids unsafe processing on incomplete or invalid input. In Composer scenarios, the mature answer is the one that fails early or branches explicitly instead of letting bad data propagate through later steps.
correta_texto: Option B

pergunta: NTO wants to avoid partial processing where only the first element of a list is handled. Which Composer concept must be configured correctly?
opcoes: For Each loops around list operations
justificativa: In MuleSoft Composer, a For Each loop is used to iterate over a collection and execute the configured actions for every item. NTO wants to avoid partial processing where only the first element of a list is handled.
correta_texto: For Each loops around list operations

pergunta: A candidate flow uses Composer to orchestrate many complex system interactions, heavy transformations and advanced error handling. What limitation should you keep in mind for the exam?
opcoes: Composer cannot interact with SaaS systems
Composer is designed for simpler logic; complex orchestration and transformations belong in Anypoint APIs
justificativa: Composer is powerful for straightforward no-code automations, but heavy transformations, advanced orchestration, and richer error handling typically belong in Anypoint APIs. That is the limitation the exam is testing here.
correta_texto: Composer is designed for simpler logic; complex orchestration and transformations belong in Anypoint APIs

pergunta: A scenario describes the need to test a Composer flow without impacting real SAP production data. Which approach is best?
opcoes: Use Test Mode in a sandbox connected to a SAP test environment
Use Test Mode directly against SAP production
justificativa: The safest testing approach is to use Composer Test Mode in a sandbox connected to a non-production SAP environment. That validates the flow without risking production records.
correta_texto: Use Test Mode in a sandbox connected to a SAP test environment

pergunta: An exam item shows three plausible options and one that bypasses all validation, sending raw data directly to the destination. What kind of trap is this?
opcoes: Alternative technically possible but unsafe or fragile
Alternative that follows all good practices
justificativa: The unsafe option may be technically possible, but it is fragile because it bypasses validation and sends raw data directly to the target system. The exam often uses this kind of trap answer.
correta_texto: Alternative technically possible but unsafe or fragile

pergunta: AnyAirlines wants to build a mission-critical, high-volume integration reused by many teams. One option is “implement everything in Composer”. Why is this likely wrong?
opcoes: Composer does not support SaaS connectors
High-volume, reusable integrations belong in Anypoint APIs, not in Composer alone
justificativa: Mission-critical, high-volume integrations reused by many teams should live in governed Anypoint APIs. Composer can consume those capabilities, but it should not be the only layer for this kind of enterprise integration.
correta_texto: High-volume, reusable integrations belong in Anypoint APIs, not in Composer alone

pergunta: NTO wants business admins to quickly connect Salesforce and Slack for simple notifications without involving developers. Which combination is most aligned with exam expectations?
justificativa: MuleSoft Composer is designed for no-code integrations using prebuilt SaaS connectors, triggers, and simple mappings. NTO wants business admins to quickly connect Salesforce and Slack for simple notifications without involving developers. It is typically preferred over custom code when requirements are straightforward and speed-to-delivery matters.
correta_texto: MuleSoft Composer + Slack connector

pergunta: A scenario states that an integration must be easily consumable by multiple channels (web, mobile, partners) and governed with policies like rate limiting. Which is the best place for the core logic?
justificativa: When multiple channels must consume the same core logic and governance policies such as rate limiting apply, the right home for that capability is Anypoint Platform APIs.
correta_texto: Anypoint Platform APIs

pergunta: An exam item offers two options, and only option B preserves reuse and cleaner architecture. Which should the candidate choose?
justificativa: Option B is correct because it keeps reusable integration logic in the shared layer instead of scattering the same logic across each consuming flow. That improves maintainability, supports future reuse, and keeps the architecture cleaner as more consumers appear.
correta_texto: B

pergunta: A candidate suggests using Composer to perform heavy data transformations that already exist in a Process API. What is the better approach?
opcoes: Reuse Process API and keep transformation centered on it
Implement all logic in Screen Flows
justificativa: If the heavy transformation logic already exists in a Process API, the right move is to reuse that API instead of duplicating business logic in Composer. That preserves consistency and reuse.
correta_texto: Reuse Process API and keep transformation centered on it

pergunta: A process uses Composer to trigger MuleSoft RPA via its connector for screen automation in a legacy system. What typical pattern does this illustrate?
opcoes: Compose as “light orchestration” and RPA as “heavy execution”
justificativa: MuleSoft RPA is the right fit when you must automate a user interface or a legacy system without usable APIs. A process uses Composer to trigger MuleSoft RPA via its connector for screen automation in a legacy system. It operates via screen-level interactions, whereas API-based approaches require stable endpoints.
correta_texto: Compose as “light orchestration” and RPA as “heavy execution”

pergunta: AnyAirlines has a Composer flow that fails frequently due to destination system unavailability. Which strategy is most reasonable in this context?
opcoes: Blindly retry infinite times on Composer
Validate data and, when possible, move critical logic to APIs with appropriate policies and retry
justificativa: If the destination system is frequently unavailable, the mature response is to validate inputs and move more critical resilience patterns into APIs where retry and policy controls are stronger. Composer alone should not carry all of that burden.
correta_texto: Validate data and, when possible, move critical logic to APIs with appropriate policies and retry

pergunta: An option claims that Composer supports complex 'try/catch' structures like a full programming language. Why is that likely a trap answer?
opcoes: Because Composer does not support any kind of logic
Because Composer has limited logic; it is recommended to validate before and treat errors in a simple way
Because Composer is just an IDE
Because Composer is just like Anypoint Studio
justificativa: That answer is a trap because Composer supports simpler logic patterns, not the kind of rich programmatic exception handling you would expect in a full coding environment. The safer approach is to validate early and keep error handling simple.
correta_texto: Because Composer has limited logic; it is recommended to validate before and treat errors in a simple way

pergunta: NTO wants to understand that integrations built by business users do not bypass corporate APIs and governance. Which guideline is most appropriate?
opcoes: Composer should always call systems directly
Composer should consume existing APIs when available, instead of going straight to the backend
Composer should never be used with APIs
justificativa: Composer should respect existing corporate APIs instead of bypassing them. That keeps governance, reuse, and policy control in place even when the flow is built by business users.
correta_texto: Composer should consume existing APIs when available, instead of going straight to the backend

pergunta: The scenario describes a one-off load of thousands of historical records. One answer suggests building a permanent Composer flow for this. Why is this potentially not ideal?
opcoes: For punctual migration, dedicated tools or temporary scripts may be more suitable
Composer has no connectors for SaaS
justificativa: For a one-time historical migration, dedicated migration tools or temporary scripts may be more appropriate than keeping a permanent Composer flow. The tool choice should match the lasting business need.
correta_texto: For punctual migration, dedicated tools or temporary scripts may be more suitable

pergunta: An exam question presents three statements, and the scenario indicates that A, B, and C are all valid together. Which answer should the candidate choose?
opcoes: A, B and C are correct and complementary
justificativa: Choose the combined answer because the scenario indicates that A, B, and C complement each other rather than compete with each other.
correta_texto: A, B and C are correct and complementary

pergunta: A candidate chooses an option where Composer directly manipulates database tables of a core system instead of calling a System API. Why is this problematic?
opcoes: Because it ignores API-led, governance and reuse of the existing System API
Because Composer does not support connections
Because System APIs cannot be versioned
justificativa: Direct database manipulation bypasses the API-led layer that should expose the system safely and consistently. It reduces governance and ignores reuse of the existing System API.
correta_texto: Because it ignores API-led, governance and reuse of the existing System API

pergunta: AnyAirlines wants to give non-technical users autonomy while still keeping control over integration changes. Which governance model is more aligned with the exam?
opcoes: Each team builds flows without any guidelines
A C4E defining patterns, reusable assets, and support for citizen integrators
Only a central team can create any integration
All integrations must be done only by Java developers
justificativa: A Center for Enablement is the balanced governance model here because it enables citizen integrators while still providing standards, reusable assets, and architectural guardrails.
correta_texto: A C4E defining patterns, reusable assets, and support for citizen integrators

pergunta: A scenario compares two solutions, and Option 1 is the only one that respects API-led layering and reuse. Which answer should the candidate choose?
opcoes: Option 1, as it respects API-led and reuse
justificativa: Option 1 is correct because API-led architecture separates system access, orchestration, and consumer-specific behavior. When a solution keeps that layering intact, reuse and change isolation improve; collapsing everything into one place usually increases coupling and makes later changes harder.
correta_texto: Option 1, as it respects API-led and reuse

pergunta: A question mixes API-led layers and tools in a way that keeps Composer consuming reusable APIs rather than bypassing them. How should this be evaluated?
opcoes: Wrong because Composer should never consume APIs
In line with the MuleSoft strategy for reuse and separation of responsibilities
Wrong as Process APIs should not exist
Wrong because RPA should replace Composer
justificativa: This is aligned with MuleSoft strategy because Composer should work with reusable APIs, not replace the API-led layers that provide governance and separation of concerns.
correta_texto: In line with the MuleSoft strategy for reuse and separation of responsibilities
